name: liblog-any-perl
title: liblog-any-perl
summary: Perl module to log messages safely and efficiently
description:
Perl module to log messages safely and efficiently
Log::Any allows CPAN modules to safely and efficiently log messages, while
letting the application choose (or decline to choose) a logging mechanism
such as Log::Dispatch or Log::Log4perl.
.
Log::Any has a very tiny footprint and no dependencies beyond Perl 5.6, which
makes it appropriate for even small CPAN modules to use. It defaults to
'null' logging activity, so a module can safely log without worrying about
whether the application has chosen (or will ever choose) a logging mechanism.
.
The application, in turn, may choose one or more logging mechanisms via
Log::Any::Adapter.
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use liblog-any-perl because Perl module to log messages safely and efficiently.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use liblog-any-perl as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair liblog-any-perl with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `liblog-any-perl --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
